rationale: z3 stalls because the two ensures-conjuncts for r1 and r2 unfold `SingleDelivery::send_single_message` on disjoint (post, sm) chains and never align the resulting `if new_seqno > params.max_seqno` case-split. I bind the shared `(pre_v, post1_v, post2_v, params)` once and restate the alignment as four implications Verus must check: (A) Some/None mismatches across runs are contradictory because the if-condition is computed from the shared `(pre_v, dst@)`, so a Some witness forces `new_seqno <= max_seqno` while a None witness forces the opposite; (B) matching Somes pin both `sm@` and `post@` to the same else-branch witness `SingleMessage::Message{ seqno: new_seqno, m: m@, dst: dst@ }`; (C) matching Nones pin both `post@` to `pre_v` via the then-branch; (D) lifts `SingleDelivery` view-equality (extensional via `#[verifier::ext_equal]`) to per-component view-equality of `receive_state` and `send_state`, which is what `CSingleDelivery::view()` projects onto each concrete field; (E) pushes that through the `map_values` view of `CTombstoneTable`/`CSendState` to the underlying `epmap@` Map views, closing as much of the gap to `det_send_single_cmessage_equal` as the spec permits.
